mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the documented unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 1% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero with a p-value greater than 0.10 during periods of rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5 percent annually forces central banks to prioritize inflation control over growth, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, which supports consumer income growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information coefficient over 60-day horizons across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product expansion directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit growth, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward regression coefficients for GDPC1 turn negative or statistically insignificant with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory destocking, which triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following a documented 1.0 percent monthly industrial production drop.
